The plant Brassica, belonging to the subfamily Crucifera, is a group of flowering plants characterized by their four-petaled flowers and a four-chambered ovary. The health benefits of Brassica plants include reducing inflammation, fighting cancer, and supporting cardiovascular health, due to their high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The bioactive constituents of Brassica plants include glucosinolates, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, as well as isothiocyanates, which have been found to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Brassica plants include teas, capsules, and extracts, often used to support digestive health and immune function.
